title: Position: Don't Just "Fix it in Post": A Science of AI Must Study Training Dynamics
summary: arXiv:2606.06533v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: What would it mean to have a scientific understanding of AI? Models are not static objects: they are snapshots of time-evolving processes shaped by data, objectives, architectures, and optimization dynamics.
content: arXiv:2606.06533v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: What would it mean to have a scientific understanding of AI? Models are not static objects: they are snapshots of time-evolving processes shaped by data, objectives, architectures, and optimization dynamics.
author: Stella Biderman, Mohammad Aflah Khan, Niloofar Mireshghallah, Catherine Arnett, Fazl Barez, Naomi Saphra

title: Are you sure? A Comprehensive and Comprehensible Survey of Uncertainty Quantification in Symbolic Regression
summary: arXiv:2606.06567v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Symbolic regression (SR) is a class of methods that systematically explore the space of mathematical functions to discover models that accurately capture the underlying relationships in a dataset.

title: Gaussian Process Latent Factor Regression for Low-Data, High-Dimensional Output Problems
summary: arXiv:2606.06576v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In the sciences, regression tasks often require predicting high-dimensional outputs from few training examples. Multi-output Gaussian processes excel in low-data regimes but typically struggle with high-dimensional outputs.
content: arXiv:2606.06576v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In the sciences, regression tasks often require predicting high-dimensional outputs from few training examples. Multi-output Gaussian processes excel in low-data regimes but typically struggle with high-dimensional outputs.
author: Edward T. Stevenson, Eric T. Wolf, Mei Ting Mak, N. J. Mayne, Miles Cranmer
